name: Pozo da Ferida – circular from Boimente
name_original: Pozo da Ferida – circular desde Boimente
region_intro: Hiking around Viveiro offers a diverse range of natural landscapes, from coastal paths along the Rías Altas to lush inland forests. The region is characterized by its prominent estuary, varied woodlands, and numerous viewpoints that provide expansive vistas. Hikers can explore a mix of terrain, including river valleys, gentle hills, and coastal trails. Best hiking trails around Viveiro The most popular hiking route is Pozo da Ferida – circular from Boimente, a difficult 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route leads to a waterfall with a natural pool, set within a natural environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuciño do Porco Point – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Covas, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This trail offers spectacular coastal vistas from the Fuciño do Porco viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove) – El Abuelo Eucalyptus Tree loop from Monumento Natural Souto da Retorta, a 1.5 miles (2.4 km) trail leading through a unique eucalyptus forest, often completed in about 38 minutes. Hiking around Viveiro is defined by its coastal paths, dense eucalyptus forests, and panoramic viewpoints overlooking the estuary. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging climbs. The routes in Viveiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 99 reviews. More than 950 hikers have used komoot to explore Viveiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Viveiro?
A: Viveiro offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy riverside strolls to more challenging climbs, ensuring there's a route for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Viveiro?
A: Yes, Viveiro has several easy and family-friendly options. A great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove) – El Abuelo Eucalyptus Tree loop from Monumento Natural Souto da Retorta&lt;/a&gt;. This 1.5-mile (2.4 km) trail takes you through a unique eucalyptus forest with towering trees and is practically flat, making it ideal for all ages. Another scenic and easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15101090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V – Covas loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers pleasant coastal views.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls in Viveiro?
A: For a hike featuring a beautiful waterfall, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979358327/pozo-da-ferida-circular-from-boimente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo da Ferida – circular from Boimente&lt;/a&gt; route. This challenging 10.8-mile (17.4 km) trail leads you to the Pozo da Ferida waterfall, which boasts a natural pool set within an extraordinary natural environment, offering a refreshing stop.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Viveiro?
A: Absolutely. Many of Viveiro's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979358327/pozo-da-ferida-circular-from-boimente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo da Ferida – circular from Boimente&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7011781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Covas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove) – El Abuelo Eucalyptus Tree loop from Monumento Natural Souto da Retorta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Viveiro?
A: Viveiro's hiking trails often lead to or pass by impressive natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the towering eucalyptus trees of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6453869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove)&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy spectacular coastal vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maiden's Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paper Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any coastal hiking paths in Viveiro?
A: Yes, Viveiro is renowned for its beautiful coastal paths. The Natural Route of the Cantabrian Path, which spans over 150 kilometers along the Galician coast, passes through Viveiro, offering numerous opportunities to explore stunning beaches and charming towns. Shorter loops around Covas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7011748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covas – Gate of Charles V loop from Covas&lt;/a&gt;, also provide excellent coastal walking experiences.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Viveiro?
A: The hiking experiences in Viveiro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 99 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic coastal views at Fuciño do Porco to the serene, ancient eucalyptus forests, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the region a pleasure.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic views of the Viveiro estuary?
A: Absolutely. Viveiro is home to several viewpoints that offer breathtaking panoramic views. The Mirador de San Roque, located 357 meters above sea level, provides expansive vistas of the Viveiro estuary, its marina, Covas beach, and the port of Celeiro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7011781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Covas&lt;/a&gt; also offers spectacular coastal panoramas.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Viveiro?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails in Viveiro, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, particularly in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Some specific natural monuments or protected areas might have restrictions, so it's best to check local signage if you are unsure.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Viveiro's hiking trails?
A: Viveiro's terrain is quite varied. You can expect a mix of coastal paths with sea views, lush inland forests featuring diverse tree species like eucalyptus, chestnuts, and oaks, and trails along river valleys. Some routes involve gentle hills, while others, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979358327/pozo-da-ferida-circular-from-boimente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo da Ferida – circular from Boimente&lt;/a&gt;, include more challenging climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Viveiro's trails often weave through areas rich in history. The Ruta de los Muíños de Balea, for example, follows the Fontecova stream and allows you to appreciate up to six well-preserved, disused mills. You can also find remnants of the significant iron mines at Minas da Silvarosa, which operated for decades and are now part of engaging circular routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Viveiro?
A: The best time for hiking in Viveiro is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less rainfall than winter and more comfortable temperatures than the peak summer months. The landscapes are vibrant, and the trails are usually in excellent condition.
tips: From the top of the Pozo da Ferida waterfall there are spectacular views of the great waterfall, approximately 30 meters. Care must be taken to access the top of the waterfall.

Impressive waterfall of the Loureiro river, a tributary of the Landro river. The waterfall is about 30 meters high and has a beautiful pond surrounded by native trees. The trail to the waterfall is very nice and easy to do as a family.

waypoints.name: View from the Top of Pozo da Ferida
